segments.text: In those days Hezekiah became ill and was at the point of death. The prophet Isaiah son of Amoz went to him and said,
Put your house in order, because you are going to die; you will not recover.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to the Lord,
Remember, Lord, how I have walked before you faithfully and with wholehearted devotion and have done what is good in your eyes.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
Then the word of the Lord came to Isaiah:
Go and tell Hezekiah, 'This is what the Lord, the God of your father David, says: I have heard your prayer and seen your tears; I will add fifteen years to your life.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria. I will defend this city.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: This is the Lord's sign to you that the Lord will do what he has promised:
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I will make the shadow cast by the sun go back the ten steps it has gone down on the stairway of Ahaz.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So the sunlight went back the ten steps it had gone down.

verses.title: A writing of Hezekiah king of Judah after his illness and recovery

segments.text: In the prime of my life must I go through the gates of death and be robbed of the rest of my years?
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: I will not again see the Lord himself in the land of the living; no longer will I look on my fellow man, or be with those who now dwell in this world.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Like a shepherd's tent my house has been pulled down and taken from me. Like a weaver I have rolled up my life, and he has cut me off from the loom; day and night you made an end of me.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: I waited patiently till dawn, but like a lion he broke all my bones; day and night you made an end of me.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: I cried like a swift or thrush, I moaned like a mourning dove. My eyes grew weak as I looked to the heavens. I am being threatened; Lord, come to my aid!
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: But what can I say? He has spoken to me, and he himself has done this. I will walk humbly all my years because of this anguish of my soul.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Lord, by such things people live; and my spirit finds life in them too. You restored me to health and let me live.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Surely it was for my benefit that I suffered such anguish. In your love you kept me from the pit of destruction; you have put all my sins behind your back.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: For the grave cannot praise you, death cannot sing your praise; those who go down to the pit cannot hope for your faithfulness.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: The living, the living—they praise you, as I am doing today; parents tell their children about your faithfulness.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: The Lord will save me, and we will sing with stringed instruments all the days of our lives in the temple of the Lord.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Isaiah had said, 'Prepare a poultice of figs and apply it to the boil, and he will recover.'
Hezekiah had asked, 'What will be the sign that I will go up to the temple of the Lord?'
